sessions.description: Description: 10min Easy RPE4 (Endurance) pace to warm up  25min Comfortably Hard RPE6 (Half Marathon) pace 10min Easy RPE4 (Endurance) pace to cool down

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Back Sculling drills w/ 10s rest between drills 4x300m Moderate-Hard, RPE7 w/ 45s rest between intervals 4x50m Fastest Average, RPE9 w/ 20s rest between sprints 8x25m Kick & Rotate drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Back Sculling (BS) - Keep your arms at your sides and point your fingertips back toward your toes as you propel yourself with those familiar figure-eight movements without moving your hands any wider than shoulder width.  Fastest Average (FA) - Swim each repetition at a pace that is as fast as you can sustain for the entire set, while still being able to complete all the repetitions at the given RPE. Aim to maintain a consistent time for each repetition throughout the set. For example: If doing 4 x 100m freestyle, it would be better to swim 1:20, 1:21, 1:19, 1:20 (with an average pace of 1:20 per 100m) than to swim 1:18, 1:18, 1:20, 1:28 (averaging 1:21 per 100m).  Kick & Rotate (K&R) - Begin kicking on your side for 3-5 seconds, then rotate onto your belly and "catch up" to your leading arm with the other arm bringing both arms out in front of you. Pull with the arm that was leading as you roll onto your opposite side. Kick for 3-5 seconds on this side, catch up with the other arm, and immediately pull the previously leading arm and rotate to the opposite side. Over time, try to spend only 2 seconds at a time kicking on each side.

TSS 19, IF 0.62, kJ(Cal) 213.  Description: Taku is a short, aerobic Endurance workout consisting of 30 minutes spent between 60-70% FTP.Taku can serve as a light workout on its own, it can be paired with a short, intense interval session, beforehand or afterward, or it can be used to get you on the bike between hard workouts in order to spin out some heaviness. Goals: Endurance workouts are aimed at improving your aerobic power production capabilities in a steady, low-stress manner. By training your endurance muscle fibers to better utilize oxygen to metabolize fat as fuel, your muscles can spare sugar stores for more intense efforts.

sessions.description: Description: Either Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 1200m Easy-Moderate, RPE5 Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4   OR compete in a B-priority practice race

TSS 59, IF 0.77, kJ(Cal) 523.  Description: Leavitt -3 is 50 continuous minutes of Tempo work broken into 7x7-minute segments ranging from 75-85% FTP. Goals: Riding at the lower end of the Sweet Spot range pushes you to the height of aerobic endurance.While rather uncomfortable, working here isn't too stressful to keep you from spending pretty long durations at a productive work level that's not too demanding in terms of recovery.This is close to where half-distance triathletes will spend multiple hours during their bike leg. This is also where ample time is spent during long road races, especially ones with steady climbs.Consider using portions or entireties of these intervals to work on riding in your aerodynamic position without sacrificing power.There is no scheduled rest during this workout, but brief, occasional backspins are encouraged if they're the difference between loss of form and a high-quality workout.Try to keep your cadence above 85rpm, preferably above 90rpm.

Description: 35min at an Easy RPE4 (Endurance) pace.   Keep inserting 1-minute walking breaks as necessary but try to increase the steady run durations for a minute or 2 more this week, maybe 6 or 7 minutes each time.

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 4x25m Fingertips & Thumbs drills w/ 10s rest between drills 7x100m Easy-Moderate, RPE5 w/ 5s rest between intervals 4x25m Kick & Rotate drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Fingertips & Thumbs (F&T) - During your normal freestyle stroke, keep your elbows high and lightly drag your fingertips across the water as your working arm recovers. Then, as your working hand exits the water on the release, lightly scrape your thumb against your thigh. These two actions can be done individually or combined.  Kick & Rotate (K&R) - Begin kicking on your side for 3-5 seconds, then rotate onto your belly and "catch up" to your leading arm with the other arm bringing both arms out in front of you. Pull with the arm that was leading as you roll onto your opposite side. Kick for 3-5 seconds on this side, catch up with the other arm, and immediately pull the previously leading arm and rotate to the opposite side. Over time, try to spend only 2 seconds at a time kicking on each side.

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Catch-Up drills w/ 10s rest between drills 5x100m of 25m Build from RPE4 to RPE8/25m Descend from RPE8 to RPE4, 10s rest between intervals 8x25m Kicking drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Catch-Up (CU) - Your lead arm will remain fully extended in front of you until your working arm completes a full stroke. The working arm now becomes the lead arm and remains fully extended in front of you until your working arm completes its full stroke and "catches up".  Kicking (K) - Swim facedown using only your feet to propel you as you breathe just as you would during a freestyle stroke by rolling to the side and turning your head to catch some air. Focus on "pushing" your chest down while keeping your legs & hips up toward the surface as you practice balance & kicking technique.
Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 1200m Easy-Moderate, RPE5 Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4

TSS 71, IF 0.84, kJ(Cal) 543.  Description: Steamboat +2 consists of 5x8-minute spin-ups between 88-94% FTP where leg speed is the focus. Each interval consists of 4 quick minutes, 3 quicker minutes, and 1 really quick minute at relatively low power output.2 minutes of recovery fall between intervals. Goals: The primary focus is Improved pedaling mechanics through the use of high-cadence efforts that cultivate a more efficient pedalstroke and allow better use of energy at all leg speeds.Higher cadences reduce the level of muscle stress and shift the energy demand more toward the aerobic system and away from the anaerobic, sugar-dependent system - many light pedalstrokes utilize less sugar than fewer hard pedalstrokes.Cadence can climb as high as a rider can sustain while exhibiting excellent control but should fall no lower than 90rpm. ILT cadence is a low as necessary to learn single-leg control with a goal cadence of 90rpm/leg for all durations as improvement takes place.

Description: 40min at an Easy RPE4 (Endurance) pace.   As the overall run duration increases a little bit, keep adding 1-minute walking breaks as necessary as you grow your steady run durations another minute or two aiming to run for 8-10 minutes at a stretch

Description: 40min at an Easy RPE4 (Endurance) pace.   Shoot for those slightly longer stretches this week but keep your form at the top of your list of priorities. Much like swimming, small improvements in your technique can go a long way toward making you faster (and less prone to injury).

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Front Sculling drills w/ 10s rest between drills 6x100m of 25m Easy, RPE4/25m Hard, RPE8, 10s rest between intervals 8x25m Side Kicking drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Front Sculling (FS) - Stretch your arms out in front of you, fingertips pointed forward, arms no more than shoulder-width apart and use both hands to propel yourself forward by "sculling" in a figure-eight pattern as you simulate the catch over & over. Use a minimal kick and do this with your head up or down.  Side Kicking (SK) - Swim on your side with your lower arm extended ahead of you and the upper arm resting against your side. Rest your head against your shoulder and look down, keeping your head completely submerged while using only your kick to propel yourself. Rotate your head slightly when inhaling and try to keep your hips & legs high throughout as you swim a full pool length before switching sides.
Description: 50min at an Easy RPE4 (Endurance) pace.   Nearly an hour of running so fluidly and lightly that it?s hard to tell you?re even working. Walk briefly when necessary and only if necessary.
Description: Warm-Up 250m Easy, RPE4 1500m Easy-Moderate, RPE5 Cool-Down 250m Easy, RPE4

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Stroke Counting drills w/ 10s rest between drills 4x100m Easy-Moderate, RPE5 w/ 5s rest between intervals Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Stroke Counting (SC) - Target your stroke efficiency by trying to reduce the number of strokes you use to reach the opposite side of the pool. Count your strokes on your first trip across, then try to reduce the number of strokes during the next 2-3 trips across the pool by taking longer, more powerful pulls, rotating more & gliding a bit longer.

TSS 18, IF 0.49, kJ(Cal) 253.  Description: Lazy Mountain -2 is 45 minutes of easy, steady effort between 45-55% FTP. Goals: Recovery rides can be used to further your aerobic base by riding very easily when your muscles are already pretty cooked. When recovery is the goal, keep the ride as short as necessary and pull the plug well inside of an hour if effort this low is wearing you down and postponing your recovery.Spin lightly and reasonably quickly, preferably above 85rpm.

TSS 27, IF 0.52, kJ(Cal) 360.  Description: Pettit -1 is an hour of light Endurance work spent mostly between 50-60% FTP. Goals: The primary aim here is to allow riders to accumulate a little extra workout stress without hampering recovery.Additionally, optional form drills target improvements in pedaling mechanics and sustainable leg speed.

Fastest Average (FA) - Swim each repetition at a pace that is as fast as you can sustain for the entire set, while still being able to complete all the repetitions at the given RPE. Aim to maintain a consistent time for each repetition throughout the set. For example: If doing 4 x 100m freestyle, it would be better to swim 1:20, 1:21, 1:19, 1:20 (with an average pace of 1:20 per 100m) than to swim 1:18, 1:18, 1:20, 1:28 (averaging 1:21 per 100m).  Kicking (K) - Swim facedown using only your feet to propel you as you breathe just as you would during a freestyle stroke by rolling to the side and turning your head to catch some air. Focus on "pushing" your chest down while keeping your legs & hips up toward the surface as you practice balance & kicking technique.

TSS 35, IF 0.53, kJ(Cal) 463.  Description: Vladeasa consists of 75 minutes of aerobic Endurance riding spent between 50-55% FTP. Goals: Aerobic Endurance workouts are aimed at improving your aerobic (oxygen-reliant) power producing capabilities in a low-stress manner. By riding for increasingly longer periods of time, your endurance muscle fibers become more efficient at utilizing oxygen and sparing sugar stores for more intense efforts.Additionally, Endurance workouts like this can improve oxygen delivery, increase muscular resistance to fatigue, improve integrity of connective tissue, and even help you improve your on-bike nutrition strategies.
Description: Warm-Up 250m Easy, RPE4 2000m Easy-Moderate, RPE5 Cool-Down 250m Easy, RPE4

TSS 82, IF 0.90, kJ(Cal) 515.  Description: Denali +1 is 4x6-minute intervals at 110-112% FTP with 6-minute recoveries following each interval.  Goals: The goals of workouts like this one are to extend supra-threshold power over greater durations by increasing both anaerobic and aerobic work capacity via a shorter, more intense approach.This power level incorporates a high anaerobic component but also a high aerobic contribution that only gets higher over the course of each reasonably long interval. This impacts both energy delivery systems in a meaningful and useful way, given adequate recovery, of course.Strive to keep your cadence above 90rpm, even closer to 100rpm if possible.

Description: Obama is 5x5-minute repeats at 107-108% FTP, each with a short, 1-minute intermediate break.Each segmented interval is separated by nearly 5-minute recovery valleys. Goals: A noticeable drop from typical VO2 Max power allows far greater time at a potentially very productive power output well above your current sustainable power.The intent is to build greater muscular endurance and mental toughness by working at a high percentage of your maximal oxygen uptake with repeats that thoroughly stress your aerobic system. Shoot for a cadence above 85rpm, above 95rpm if possible.

TSS 119, IF 0.89, kJ(Cal) 789.  Description: Warlow +2 is 5x9-minute over-under intervals alternating between 2 minutes pushed up to 98% FTP and 1 minute still at 115% FTP with 6-minute recoveries between intervals. Goals: This workout chiefly aims to develop your ability to handle changes in pace while maintaining a high level of work and enhance muscle endurance in the process.The surges flood the muscle with metabolic byproducts bringing with them the desire to ease up, don't. Hold that burn, then back off only slightly during the valleys in order to work through the burn and effectively train your muscles to process the buildup while still doing a lot of work.These intervals can increase your ability to better process lactate thereby increasing not only how long you can work above FTP but also increasing FTP itself.Try to keep your cadence in the 85-95rpm range whether enduring a valley or powering through a short surge.

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Sighting drills w/ 10s rest between drills 4x300m Moderate-Hard, RPE7 w/ 30s rest between intervals 4x50m Fastest Average, RPE9 w/ 20s rest between sprints 8x25m Kicking drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Sighting (S) - Instead of turning your head to the side to breathe, raise your head to look forward and "sight" a landmark toward which you'll swim. Breathe just as you would when turning to the side by inhaling before lowering your head, exhaling underwater, and learn to disrupt your stroke as minimally as possible in the process - eventually not at all.  Fastest Average (FA) - Swim each repetition at a pace that is as fast as you can sustain for the entire set, while still being able to complete all the repetitions at the given RPE. Aim to maintain a consistent time for each repetition throughout the set. For example: If doing 4 x 100m freestyle, it would be better to swim 1:20, 1:21, 1:19, 1:20 (with an average pace of 1:20 per 100m) than to swim 1:18, 1:18, 1:20, 1:28 (averaging 1:21 per 100m).  Kicking (K) - Swim facedown using only your feet to propel you as you breathe just as you would during a freestyle stroke by rolling to the side and turning your head to catch some air. Focus on "pushing" your chest down while keeping your legs & hips up toward the surface as you practice balance & kicking technique.

TSS 98, IF 0.88, kJ(Cal) 694.  Description: Carpathian Peak +1 is 3x15-minute over-under intervals with 1-minute valleys at 95% FTP and 2-minute peaks that ascend to 105% FTP.Recoveries between intervals are 7 minutes long. Goals: The primary objective of over-unders is to increase your ability to tolerate and utilize the byproducts that accompany riding above your FTP, all while maintaining a reasonably high power output. In doing so, you'll develop the ability to handle changes in pace while doing quite a lot of work. The over-segments or 'surges' flood the muscle with metabolic byproducts bringing with them the desire to ease up - don't. Hold that burn, then back off only slightly during the under-segments or 'valleys' in order to work through the burn and effectively train your muscles to process the lactic buildup while still going pretty hard.These intervals can increase how long you can work above FTP as well as how quickly you can reprocess the metabolic buildup that can limit your capabilities. Equally as important, the short efforts above your current FTP can actually lead to increases in FTP.Try to keep your cadence above 85rpm, preferably above 95rpm.

Goals: Aerobic Endurance workouts are aimed at improving your aerobic-power-producing capabilities in a low stress manner.By riding for increasingly longer periods of time, your endurance muscle fibers become more efficient at utilizing fat for fuel and sparing sugar stores for more intense efforts.Additionally, Endurance workouts like this can improve oxygen delivery & utilization, increase muscular resistance to fatigue, improve integrity of connective tissue, and even help you improve your on-bike nutrition strategies.Regarding the form drills, Pettit targets improvements in pedaling mechanics, leg speed, and sprinting form.

TSS 64, IF 0.80, kJ(Cal) 522.  Description: Rendezvous is 50 minutes of continuous progression from 60% FTP all the way up to 100% FTP via 10% increases every 10 minutes.  Goals: Progression workouts ever so gradually increase the power requirement until you are working at your highest sustainable power, all in an effort to raise your muscular endurance, grow your ability to withstand increasing physical discomfort and your increase your time to exhaustion.By gradually raising your power and along with it your fatigue, acidic accumulation and lactate levels, the continually increasing power demand will serve as the stimulus for subsequent adaptation. Translation: this workout + proper recovery = more power for longer spans of time.Do your best to keep your cadence in that 85-95rpm range with each upward step in power output, and spend greater lengths of time in your aero position if your discipline requires riding low.

TSS 64, IF 0.71, kJ(Cal) 613.  Description: Mont Gosford is 1 hour and 15 minutes of Tempo intervals spent between 70-80% FTP with intermittent, 2 to 3-minute recoveries at 55% FTP.  Goals: Outside of the climbing goals of refining your climbing technique & familiarizing your body with the somewhat different stress of climbing, the additional aim here is to continue building aerobic power.Try to employ realistic climbing cadences if you're simulating hill climbs. Ideally, seated climbing should be done above 80rpm and try to stay above 70rpm when standing. But if you know the particular demands of the climbs you'll face, feel free to use suitable cadences specific to your needs.

TSS 79, IF 0.80, kJ(Cal) 649.  Description: Piute is a progression workout where each 10% step lasts 12 minute and starts at 60% FTP and then progresses the workload up to 100% FTP during the final step.  Goals: The muscular endurance intervals in this particular sequence of workouts - consisting of Rendezvous, Piute, Ragged & Tower - grow in duration each workout via slightly longer steps. Progression Workouts ever so gradually increase the power requirement until you are working at your Functional Threshold Power, all in an effort to raise that maximum steady-state pace, your ability to withstand increasing physical discomfort, and your time to exhaustion.Do your best to keep your cadence in that 85-95rpm range with each upward step in power output, and spend greater lengths of time in your aero position even as the power requirements grow.

TSS 96, IF 0.69, kJ(Cal) 956.  Description: Perkins is a highly varied 2 hours of aerobic Endurance riding ranging from 50-80% FTP. Goals: The work here targets improvements in aerobic endurance/aerobic base by asking you to work at a moderate intensities well below your FTP. By sustaining this effort level for fairly long and sometimes quite long, minimally interrupted periods of time, your body will improve its aerobic capabilities thereby improving your level of power output at low to moderate levels of exertion. Greater aerobic capbilities means you can work harder before dipping very far into your precious sugar stores thereby preserving those stores for the higher-intensity efforts. This also means you'll require less food/carbohydrate to fuel your effort during your events.Try to maintain a cadence between 85-95rpm.

TSS 59, IF 0.89, kJ(Cal) 393.  Description: Adams is reduced-load assortment of suprathreshold efforts consisting of 7 minutes at 105% FTP, 5 minutes at 108% FTP, 3 minutes at 115% FTP and 2 minutes at 121% FTP.3-minute recoveries separate all intervals. Goals: The aim here is to keep increasing the demand placed on your metabolic capabilities with the intent of furthering your sustainable, repeatable high-end power output.Shoot for a cadence above 85rpm on the longer intervals, ideally above 95rpm on the shorter ones.
Description: Warm-Up 200m Easy, RPE4 2400m Easy-Moderate, RPE5 Cool-Down 200m Easy, RPE4

TSS 59, IF 0.77, kJ(Cal) 500.  Description: Slide Mountain is 3x10-minute intervals at 85% FTP with 4-minute recoveries between intervals. Goals: The primary aim of Tempo work is to improve your ability to resist fatigue at reasonably high power outputs over substantial lengths of time.In other words, workouts like this are all about increasing your muscular endurance.In doing so, you can improve your ability to climb faster and longer, face stiff winds without fading, and time trial more consistently over the entire course of an event. Additionally, you have the option of targeting specific cadences with the aim of increasing your speed endurance as well as improving your ability to pedal properly during slower spins.Cadence recommendations vary depending on the current drill, but riders choosing to omit the drills should aim for a spin between 85-95rpm.

TSS 33, IF 0.81, kJ(Cal) 239.  Description: Marys is 2x6-minute intervals at 98% FTP with 5-minutes of recovery in between. Goals: The primary objective here is improvement in muscular endurance accomplished by growing your ability to repeat efforts at high percentages of your FTP.By working close to but below your FTP, you not only accumulate a lot of time at a meaningful level of stress, but recovery from slightly sub-threshold effort comes much more readily than recovery following work done at or just above your FTP.Try to hold your spin above 85rpm and even as high as 105rpm depending on your sustainable power and cadence goals.

Description:  Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Bilateral Breathing drills w/ 10s rest between drills 6x100m Easy-Moderate, RPE5 w/ 5s rest between intervals 5x150m Hard, RPE8 w/ 75s rest between intervals 8x25m Kick & Rotate drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Bilateral Breathing (BB) - Swim your regular freestyle stroke while breathing during every third stroke forcing yourself to discover and improve your weaker side. If this is challenging & uncoordinated at first, hang in there - your clumsy side will improve with practice.  Kick & Rotate (K&R) - Begin kicking on your side for 3-5 seconds, then rotate onto your belly and "catch up" to your leading arm with the other arm bringing both arms out in front of you. Pull with the arm that was leading as you roll onto your opposite side. Kick for 3-5 seconds on this side, catch up with the other arm, and immediately pull the previously leading arm and rotate to the opposite side. Over time, try to spend only 2 seconds at a time kicking on each side.

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Single-Arm drills w/ 10s rest between drills 5x100m Easy-Moderate, RPE5 w/ 5s rest between intervals Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Single-Arm (SA) - Restrict yourself to one arm?s use for an entire pool length by keeping the opposite arm extended in front of you or at your side (advanced). If necessary, perform these drills using fins until you improve to the point that you can eliminate them. Try to use a moderate kick and normal body positioning, rolling into your working arm.

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Back Sculling drills w/ 10s rest between drills 4x300m Moderate-Hard, RPE7 w/ 45s rest between intervals 4x50m Fastest Average, RPE9 w/ 20s rest between sprints 8x25m Kick & Rotate drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Back Sculling (BS) - Keep your arms at your sides and point your fingertips back toward your toes as you propel yourself with those familiar figure-eight movements without moving your hands any wider than shoulder width.  Fastest Average (FA) - Swim each repetition at a pace that is as fast as you can sustain for the entire set, while still being able to complete all the repetitions at the given RPE. Aim to maintain a consistent time for each repetition throughout the set. For example: If doing 4 x 100m freestyle, it would be better to swim 1:20, 1:21, 1:19, 1:20 (with an average pace of 1:20 per 100m) than to swim 1:18, 1:18, 1:20, 1:28 (averaging 1:21 per 100m).  Kick & Rotate (K&R) - Begin kicking on your side for 3-5 seconds, then rotate onto your belly and "catch up" to your leading arm with the other arm bringing both arms out in front of you. Pull with the arm that was leading as you roll onto your opposite side. Kick for 3-5 seconds on this side, catch up with the other arm, and immediately pull the previously leading arm and rotate to the opposite side. Over time, try to spend only 2 seconds at a time kicking on each side.

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Stroke Counting drills w/ 10s rest between drills 7x100m Easy-Moderate, RPE5 w/ 5s rest between intervals 5x100m Hard, RPE8 w/ 60s rest between intervals 8x25m Side Kicking drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Stroke Counting (SC) - Target your stroke efficiency by trying to reduce the number of strokes you use to reach the opposite side of the pool. Count your strokes on your first trip across, then try to reduce the number of strokes during the next 2-3 trips across the pool by taking longer, more powerful pulls, rotating more & gliding a bit longer.  Side Kicking (SK) - Swim on your side with your lower arm extended ahead of you and the upper arm resting against your side. Rest your head against your shoulder and look down, keeping your head completely submerged while using only your kick to propel yourself. Rotate your head slightly when inhaling and try to keep your hips & legs high throughout as you swim a full pool length before switching sides.

TSS 73, IF 0.85, kJ(Cal) 490.  Description: Mount Foraker -1 is 4x5-minute intervals between 105-109% FTP with 5-minute recoveries between intervals. Goals: No two ways about it, these are hard. Holding power above FTP is, by its nature, short-term due to the fact that it's a tough place to reside.But by working at mildly suprathreshold power outputs, ones that at take place at high percentages of your VO2max, you can push your FTP higher while gaining some meaningful aerobic adaptation in the process.On top of that, intervals that induce and sustain high levels of lactate work well toward lifting your physical (and mental) lactate tolerance.Strive for a cadence above 85rpm, preferably one above 95rpm.

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Bilateral Breathing drills w/ 10s rest between drills 6x100m Easy-Moderate, RPE 5 10s rest between intervals Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Bilateral Breathing (BB) - Swim your regular freestyle stroke while breathing during every third stroke forcing yourself to discover and improve your weaker side. TSS 20, IF 0.45, kJ(Cal) 313.  Description: Lazy Mountain -1 is 1 hour of easy, steady effort at a steady 45% FTP. Goals: Recovery rides can be used to further your aerobic base by riding very easily when your muscles are already pretty cooked. When recovery is the goal, keep the ride as short as necessary and pull the plug well inside of an hour if effort this low is wearing you down and postponing your recovery.Spin lightly and reasonably quickly, preferably above 85rpm.

TSS 35, IF 0.53, kJ(Cal) 463.  Description: Vladeasa consists of 75 minutes of aerobic Endurance riding spent between 50-55% FTP. Goals: Aerobic Endurance workouts are aimed at improving your aerobic (oxygen-reliant) power producing capabilities in a low-stress manner. By riding for increasingly longer periods of time, your endurance muscle fibers become more efficient at utilizing oxygen and sparing sugar stores for more intense efforts.Additionally, Endurance workouts like this can improve oxygen delivery, increase muscular resistance to fatigue, improve integrity of connective tissue, and even help you improve your on-bike nutrition strategies.

TSS 24, IF 0.49, kJ(Cal) 340.  Description: Lazy Mountain is 1 hour of easy, steady effort between 45-55% FTP. When recovery is the goal, keep the ride as short as necessary and pull the plug well inside of an hour if effort this low is wearing you down and postponing your recovery.Spin lightly and reasonably quickly, preferably above 85rpm.

If this is challenging & uncoordinated at first, hang in there - your clumsy side will improve with practice. Description: 45min at an Easy RPE4 (Endurance) pace.   Again, walk briefly when necessary but try to grow your continuous running stretches a minute or two each week. So if you ran for 8 minutes last week before walking for a minute, try to run for 9 or 10 minutes before walking during next week?s base runs.

TSS 80, IF 0.80, kJ(Cal) 665.  Description: In total, Sukaisan is 54 minutes of  Tempo/Sweet Spot work broken into 6 segments ranging 8-10 minutes and from 84-89% FTP with only short, 1-minute recoveries between each, and a 2.5-minute recovery halfway through. Goals: Tempo rides are the height of aerobic endurance and while rather uncomfortable, they aren't too stressful to keep you from spending pretty long durations at a productive work level that isn't too demanding in terms of recovery.This is where some long-course and Olympic-distance triathletes will spend ample time. This is also where time is spent during any long but aggressive road ride.Use portions or entireties of these intervals to work on riding in your aerodynamic position without sacrificing power.There is no scheduled rest during this workout, but brief, occasional backspins are encouraged if they're the difference between loss of form and a high-quality workout.        Try to do so with a cadence above 85rpm, preferably above 90rpm.

Description: 40min at an Easy RPE4 (Endurance) pace.   Keep the walking breaks as part of your process if they?re helping you maintain high run quality, but avoid using them as a crutch or an excuse to take a break. Use them if necessary but keep your running as continuous as possible if you?re capable.

TSS 60, IF 0.78, kJ(Cal) 525.  Description: Pioneer -1 is 60 minutes of near-continuous riding where you'll spend 45 minutes between 80-85% FTP, but 2x30-second recoveries break up the otherwise continuous effort. Goals: Tempo rides are the height of aerobic endurance and while rather uncomfortable, they aren't too stressful to keep you from spending pretty long durations at a productive work level that isn't too demanding in terms of recovery.This is where most long-course triathletes will live for hours at a time. This is also where a lot of time is spent during any long road ride.Use portions or entireties of these intervals to work on riding in your aerodynamic position without sacrificing power. Try to do so with a cadence above 85rpm, preferably above 90rpm.      A small handful of short, scheduled recoveries are woven into this ride in the hopes of keeping the workout quality high by helping you avoid a slow decline into poor form and spotty power output.

TSS 73, IF 0.86, kJ(Cal) 567.  Description: Tallac -1 is 3x15-minute efforts spent in the Sweet Spot (88-94% FTP) with a  brief, 1-minute intermediate recovery toward the middle of each long interval.Short, 2-minute recoveries fall between intervals. Goals: Tallac's primary aim is to increase muscular endurance via Sweet Spot efforts by expanding your base for more intense workouts later while at the same time increasing power output & endurance at moderate intensities.Various pedaling drills are incorporated into several intervals in order to target pedal economy and to alleviate some muscle stress with a quick, lower-force spin. Try to keep your spin above 85rpm and follow the on-screen pedaling drills to the best of your ability.
Description: Warm-Up 250m Easy, RPE4 1200m Easy-Moderate, RPE5 Cool-Down 250m Easy, RPE4

Description: 40min at an Easy RPE4 (Endurance) pace.   Again, walk briefly when necessary and try to recognize that quick walking breaks of a single minute can often keep the form high while costing very little in terms of aerobic fitness improvements.

TSS 81, IF 0.80, kJ(Cal) 677.  Description: In total, Chopaka +2 is 60 minutes of  Tempo/Sweet Spot work broken into 6x10-minute segments ranging from 80-90% FTP with only short, 30-second recoveries between each. Goals: Tempo rides are the height of aerobic endurance and while rather uncomfortable, they aren't too stressful to keep you from spending pretty long durations at a productive work level that isn't too demanding in terms of recovery.This is where some long-course and Olympic-distance triathletes will spend ample time. This is also where time is spent during any long but aggressive road ride.Use portions or entireties of these intervals to work on riding in your aerodynamic position without sacrificing power.There is no scheduled rest during this workout, but brief, occasional backspins are encouraged if they're the difference between loss of form and a high-quality workout.        Try to do so with a cadence above 85rpm, preferably above 90rpm.

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Fists drills w/ 10s rest between drills 4x100m of 25m Easy, RPE4/25m Hard, RPE8, 10s rest between intervals 8x25m Side Kicking drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Fists (F) - Swim with clenched fists and focus on rotating your shoulders and bending your working elbow during the catch, creating a pull that's nearly as powerful as one done with an open hand.  Side Kicking (SK) - Swim on your side with your lower arm extended ahead of you and the upper arm resting against your side. Rest your head against your shoulder and look down, keeping your head completely submerged while using only your kick to propel yourself. Rotate your head slightly when inhaling and try to keep your hips & legs high throughout as you swim a full pool length before switching sides.

Description: 35min at an Easy RPE4 (Endurance) pace.   Again, walk briefly when necessary if it means the difference between running well and sacrificing form while running efficiency suffers. Quick walking breaks of a single minute can often keep the form high while costing very little in terms of aerobic fitness improvements.

Description: 35min at an Easy RPE4 (Endurance) pace.   Feel free to insert 1 minute of walking whenever necessary as you try to run more continuously from week to week and emphasize quality over quantity at this early point in your running. Try to run for 5 minutes between walking breaks if possible.

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 4x25m Catch-Up drills w/ 10s rest between drills 6x100m Easy-Moderate, RPE5 w/ 5s rest between intervals 4x25m Kicking drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Catch-Up (CU) - Your lead arm will remain fully extended in front of you until your working arm completes a full stroke. The working arm now becomes the lead arm and remains fully extended in front of you until your working arm completes its full stroke and "catches up".  Kicking (K) - Swim facedown using only your feet to propel you as you breathe just as you would during a freestyle stroke by rolling to the side and turning your head to catch some air. Focus on "pushing" your chest down while keeping your legs & hips up toward the surface as you practice balance & kicking technique.

TSS 66, IF 0.81, kJ(Cal) 537.  Description: Grampians is basically 3x14-minute sets of Sweet Spot work performed between 85-90% FTP with 3 very short, 10-second recovery valleys thrown in to break things up. Recoveries between blocks of work are a scant 1 minute long. Goals: The primary aim of Sweet Spot work is to improve your ability to resist fatigue at reasonably high power outputs over substantial lengths of time.In other words, workouts like this are all about increasing your muscular endurance.In doing so, you can improve your ability to climb faster and longer, face stiff winds without fading and time trial more consistently over the entire course of an event. Try to keep your spin above 90rpm, even above 100rpm if a quicker spin suits you.

TSS 25, IF 0.50, kJ(Cal) 350.  Description: The Recess rides range anywhere from 20-60 minutes in duration at roughly 50% FTP.Exhausted rides, also referred to as "accumulation rides" are performed within 4-24 hours after your last key workout, race/event, or any other similar, stressful ride. These rides should leave you no more fatigued than you were before you hopped on your bike and there are no strict rules governing effort level as long as these feel recuperative and do not negatively impact your next key workout. Goals: The goals behind the Recess rides are rather wide-ranging and include adaptations such as increased resistance to muscle damage, enhanced neuromuscular coordination (i.e. improved pedal economy), increased fat metabolism, and heat acclimation to name a few.

Description: 100min at an Easy RPE4 (Endurance) pace.

TSS 87, IF 0.84, kJ(Cal) 654.  Description: Harding is 3x10-minute over-under intervals alternating between 3 minutes at 95% FTP and 2 minutes at 105-110% FTP with 6-minute recoveries between intervals.The set of over-unders is sandwiched by 2x10-minute Endurance intervals spent at 65% FTP.  Goals: The primary objective of over-unders is to increase your ability to tolerate and utilize the byproducts that accompany riding above your FTP, all while maintaining a reasonably high power output. In doing so, you?ll develop the ability to handle changes in pace while doing quite a lot of work. These intervals can increase how long you can work above FTP as well as how quickly you can reprocess the lactate buildup that can lead to muscle acidity. Equally as important, the short efforts above your current FTP can actually lead to increases in FTP.The Endurance intervals are aimed at improving your aerobic power production capabilities in a steady, low-stress manner. By training your endurance muscle fibers to better utilize oxygen to metabolize fat as fuel, your muscles can spare sugar stores for more intense efforts.This long/slow approach to fostering aerobic capabilities is the lighter, kinder alternative to high-intensity repeats, but both approaches target many of the same adaptations.Try to keep your cadence above 85rpm, preferably above 95rpm.

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Sighting drills w/ 10s rest between drills 4x300m Moderate-Hard, RPE7 w/ 30s rest between intervals 4x50m Fastest Average, RPE9 w/ 20s rest between sprints 8x25m Kicking drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Sighting (S) - Instead of turning your head to the side to breathe, raise your head to look forward and "sight" a landmark toward which you'll swim. Breathe just as you would when turning to the side by inhaling before lowering your head, exhaling underwater, and learn to disrupt your stroke as minimally as possible in the process - eventually not at all.  Fastest Average (FA) - Swim each repetition at a pace that is as fast as you can sustain for the entire set, while still being able to complete all the repetitions at the given RPE. Aim to maintain a consistent time for each repetition throughout the set. For example: If doing 4 x 100m freestyle, it would be better to swim 1:20, 1:21, 1:19, 1:20 (with an average pace of 1:20 per 100m) than to swim 1:18, 1:18, 1:20, 1:28 (averaging 1:21 per 100m).  Kicking (K) - Swim facedown using only your feet to propel you as you breathe just as you would during a freestyle stroke by rolling to the side and turning your head to catch some air. Focus on "pushing" your chest down while keeping your legs & hips up toward the surface as you practice balance & kicking technique.

Goals: Aerobic Endurance workouts are aimed at improving your aerobic (oxygen-reliant) power producing capabilities in a low-stress manner. By riding for increasingly longer periods of time, your endurance muscle fibers become more efficient at utilizing oxygen and sparing sugar stores for more intense efforts.Additionally, Endurance workouts like this can improve oxygen delivery, increase muscular resistance to fatigue, improve integrity of connective tissue, and even help you improve your on-bike nutrition strategies.

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Front Sculling drills w/ 10s rest between drills 7x100m Easy-Moderate, RPE5 w/ 5s rest between intervals 6x100m Hard, RPE8 w/ 60s rest between intervals 8x25m Kick & Rotate drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Front Sculling (FS) - Stretch your arms out in front of you, fingertips pointed forward, arms no more than shoulder-width apart and use both hands to propel yourself forward by "sculling" in a figure-eight pattern as you simulate the catch over & over. Use a minimal kick and do this with your head up or down.  Kick & Rotate (K&R) - Begin kicking on your side for 3-5 seconds, then rotate onto your belly and "catch up" to your leading arm with the other arm bringing both arms out in front of you. Pull with the arm that was leading as you roll onto your opposite side. Kick for 3-5 seconds on this side, catch up with the other arm, and immediately pull the previously leading arm and rotate to the opposite side. Over time, try to spend only 2 seconds at a time kicking on each side.

TSS 96, IF 0.80, kJ(Cal) 689.  Description: Elbow consists of 4x8.5-minute segments ranging from 99-100% FTP, each separated by 8.5-minute recoveries. Goals: The goal here is to elevate your strength endurance by learning to ride for longer and longer durations at your current FTP. As your body grows accustomed to a certain level of stress, your ability to withstand that stress for longer periods of time improves. Before you know it, your muscles don't load up like they used to, your mind isn't completely consumed with the desire to ease up, and your so-called "hour power" gravitates toward truer hour-power.The recommended cadence range is wide, 85-105 rpm, and accommodates many styles of riding. Riders wishing to focus more on improvements in strength endurance will spin toward the lower end of this cadence range while riders looking to foster a quicker spin will ride toward the upper end of the range.Or mix and match from interval to interval, just do your best to keep the power output smooth and consistent over the course of these 4 intervals.

Description: 30min at a Comfortable RP5 (Marathon) pace.   After your ride, transition quickly into your running gear and make 30 minutes of running look downright effortless...even if it doesn?t feel that way.

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Chest Sculling drills w/ 10s rest between drills 3x400m Moderate-Hard, RPE7 w/ 45s rest between intervals 6x50m Fastest Average, RPE9 w/ 20s rest between sprints 8x25m Side Kicking drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Chest Sculling (CS) - With your chest facing down, bend your elbows and point your fingertips down while keeping your elbows high. Make the same figure-eight patterns with both hands to propel yourself forward, effectively repeating the center of the stroke.  Fastest Average (FA) - Swim each repetition at a pace that is as fast as you can sustain for the entire set, while still being able to complete all the repetitions at the given RPE. Aim to maintain a consistent time for each repetition throughout the set. For example: If doing 4 x 100m freestyle, it would be better to swim 1:20, 1:21, 1:19, 1:20 (with an average pace of 1:20 per 100m) than to swim 1:18, 1:18, 1:20, 1:28 (averaging 1:21 per 100m).  Side Kicking (SK) - Swim on your side with your lower arm extended ahead of you and the upper arm resting against your side. Rest your head against your shoulder and look down, keeping your head completely submerged while using only your kick to propel yourself. Rotate your head slightly when inhaling and try to keep your hips & legs high throughout as you swim a full pool length before switching sides.

TSS 79, IF 0.89, kJ(Cal) 558.  Description: Washington -2 is 4x7-minute intervals at 105% FTP including brief, 30-second recoveries in the middle of each interval and short, 2-minute recoveries between intervals. Goals: The goal of slightly suprathreshold workouts is to extend high power over greater durations. By riding only slightly above your current FTP, you'll become accustomed - both physically and mentally - to a workload just beyond your current sustainable reach. These intervals are also an ideal time to begin bridging the gap between power in an upright riding position and power in an aerodynamic position.Strive to keep your cadence above 85rpm, preferably above 95rpm.

Recoveries between intervals are all 3 minutes long. Goals: When your fitness nears its peak, it's beneficial to combine numerous types of suprathreshold and VO2max intervals in a more race-realistic format. The aim here is to keep increasing the demand placed on your metabolic capabilities with the intent of furthering your sustainable, repeatable high-end power output.Shoot for a cadence above 85rpm on the longer intervals, ideally above 95rpm on the shorter ones.

TSS 83, IF 0.74, kJ(Cal) 767.  Description: Echo is 80 total minutes of Tempo work broken into 8x10-minute segments ranging from 70-80% FTP. Goals: Tempo rides are the height of aerobic endurance and while rather uncomfortable, they aren't too stressful to keep you from spending pretty long durations at a productive work level that isn't too demanding in terms of recovery.This is where many ultra-distance triathletes will live for hours at a time. This is also where a lot of time is spent during any long road ride. If applicable, use portions or entireties of these intervals to work on riding in your aerodynamic position without sacrificing power.There is no scheduled rest during this workout, but brief, occasional backpedal breaks are encouraged if they're the difference between loss of form and a high-quality workout.Try to do so with a cadence above 85rpm, preferably above 90rpm.

Use a minimal kick and do this with your head up or down.  Side Kicking (SK) - Swim on your side with your lower arm extended ahead of you and the upper arm resting against your side. Rest your head against your shoulder and look down, keeping your head completely submerged while using only your kick to propel yourself. Rotate your head slightly when inhaling and try to keep your hips & legs high throughout as you swim a full pool length before switching sides.

TSS 69, IF 0.83, kJ(Cal) 485.  Description: Mansfield is 4x5-minute intervals at 105% FTP with 5-minute recoveries following each interval.  Goals: The primary goal here is to improve muscular endurance while climbing and to reinforce high levels of muscle control during slower, high-force efforts.Additionally, the optional drills target improvements in your ability to not only transition in and out of the saddle smoothly, but to do so while limiting fluctuations in power output.Finally, you'll have the option to work on refining your body positioning when standing as well as improve your pedaling technique when riding out of the saddle.
Description: 40min at a Hard RPE7 (10k) pace.   Transition off the bike into the pace you plan to hit on race day.

TSS 84, IF 0.82, kJ(Cal) 679.  Description: Ragged is a progression workout where each 10% step lasts 14 minute and starts at 60% FTP and then progresses the workload up to 100% FTP during the final step.  Goals: The muscular endurance intervals in this particular sequence of workouts - consisting of Rendezvous, Piute, Ragged & Tower - grow in duration each workout via slightly longer steps. Progression Workouts ever so gradually increase the power requirement until you are working at your Functional Threshold Power, all in an effort to raise that maximum steady-state pace, your ability to withstand increasing physical discomfort, and your time to exhaustion.Do your best to keep your cadence in that 85-95rpm range with each upward step in power output, and spend greater lengths of time in your aero position even as the power requirements grow.

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Fingertips & Thumbs drills w/ 10s rest between drills 4x100m Easy-Moderate, RPE5 w/ 5s rest between intervals 4x150m Hard, RPE8 w/ 45s rest between intervals 8x25m Kicking drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Fingertips & Thumbs (F&T) - During your normal freestyle stroke, keep your elbows high and lightly drag your fingertips across the water as your working arm recovers. Then, as your working hand exits the water on the release, lightly scrape your thumb against your thigh. These two actions can be done individually or combined.  Kicking (K) - Swim facedown using only your feet to propel you as you breathe just as you would during a freestyle stroke by rolling to the side and turning your head to catch some air. Focus on "pushing" your chest down while keeping your legs & hips up toward the surface as you practice balance & kicking technique.

Description: Bald is 60 minutes of aerobic Endurance riding spent between 65-70% FTP.Optional form drills include some Endurance Spinning, some Single-Leg Focus & some Kick & Pull form work. Goals: Aerobic Endurance workouts are aimed at improving your aerobic (i.e. non-sugar) power-producing capabilities in a low-stress manner.By riding for increasingly longer periods of time, your endurance muscle fibers become more efficient at utilizing oxygen and sparing sugar stores for more intense efforts.Also, some of the shorter Endurance workouts are aimed primarily at keeping you on the bike as a form of active recovery during recovery days or weeks.

Description: Taku is a short, aerobic Endurance workout consisting of 30 minutes spent between 60-70% FTP.Taku can serve as a light workout on its own, it can be paired with a short, intense interval session, beforehand or afterward, or it can be used to get you on the bike between hard workouts in order to spin out some heaviness. By training your endurance muscle fibers to better utilize oxygen to metabolize fat as fuel, your muscles can spare sugar stores for more intense efforts.

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Fists drills w/ 10s rest between drills 3x400m Moderate-Hard, RPE7 w/ 75s rest between intervals 5x50m Fastest Average, RPE9 w/ 20s rest between sprints 8x25m Kicking drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Fists (F) - Swim with clenched fists and focus on rotating your shoulders and bending your working elbow during the catch, creating a pull that's nearly as powerful as one done with an open hand.  Fastest Average (FA) - Swim each repetition at a pace that is as fast as you can sustain for the entire set, while still being able to complete all the repetitions at the given RPE. Aim to maintain a consistent time for each repetition throughout the set. For example: If doing 4 x 100m freestyle, it would be better to swim 1:20, 1:21, 1:19, 1:20 (with an average pace of 1:20 per 100m) than to swim 1:18, 1:18, 1:20, 1:28 (averaging 1:21 per 100m).  Kicking (K) - Swim facedown using only your feet to propel you as you breathe just as you would during a freestyle stroke by rolling to the side and turning your head to catch some air. Focus on "pushing" your chest down while keeping your legs & hips up toward the surface as you practice balance & kicking technique.

Count your strokes on your first trip across, then try to reduce the number of strokes during the next 2-3 trips across the pool by taking longer, more powerful pulls, rotating more & gliding a bit longer. Aim to maintain a consistent time for each repetition throughout the set. Kicking (K) - Swim facedown using only your feet to propel you as you breathe just as you would during a freestyle stroke by rolling to the side and turning your head to catch some air. Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Stroke Counting drills w/ 10s rest between drills 6x100m Easy-Moderate, RPE5 w/ 5s rest between intervals 6x150m Hard, RPE8 w/ 45s rest between intervals 8x25m Kick & Rotate drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Stroke Counting (SC) - Target your stroke efficiency by trying to reduce the number of strokes you use to reach the opposite side of the pool. Count your strokes on your first trip across, then try to reduce the number of strokes during the next 2-3 trips across the pool by taking longer, more powerful pulls, rotating more & gliding a bit longer.  Kick & Rotate (K&R) - Begin kicking on your side for 3-5 seconds, then rotate onto your belly and "catch up" to your leading arm with the other arm bringing both arms out in front of you. Pull with the arm that was leading as you roll onto your opposite side. Kick for 3-5 seconds on this side, catch up with the other arm, and immediately pull the previously leading arm and rotate to the opposite side. Over time, try to spend only 2 seconds at a time kicking on each side.

Description: Warm-Up 300m Easy, RPE4 8x25m Catch-Up drills w/ 10s rest between drills 4x250m Moderate-Hard, RPE7 w/ 30s rest between intervals 4x50m Fastest Average, RPE9 w/ 20s rest between sprints 8x25m Kick & Rotate drills w/ 15s rest between drills Cool-Down 300m Easy, RPE4  Drill Instructions Catch-Up (CU) - Your lead arm will remain fully extended in front of you until your working arm completes a full stroke. The working arm now becomes the lead arm and remains fully extended in front of you until your working arm completes its full stroke and "catches up".  Fastest Average (FA) - Swim each repetition at a pace that is as fast as you can sustain for the entire set, while still being able to complete all the repetitions at the given RPE. Aim to maintain a consistent time for each repetition throughout the set. For example: If doing 4 x 100m freestyle, it would be better to swim 1:20, 1:21, 1:19, 1:20 (with an average pace of 1:20 per 100m) than to swim 1:18, 1:18, 1:20, 1:28 (averaging 1:21 per 100m).  Kick & Rotate (K&R) - Begin kicking on your side for 3-5 seconds, then rotate onto your belly and "catch up" to your leading arm with the other arm bringing both arms out in front of you. Pull with the arm that was leading as you roll onto your opposite side. Kick for 3-5 seconds on this side, catch up with the other arm, and immediately pull the previously leading arm and rotate to the opposite side. Over time, try to spend only 2 seconds at a time kicking on each side.
